articles.summary: Netanyahu: Israeli forces to stay in Lebanon ‘as long as need requires’ Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Thursday that the war with Iran is not over and that he intends to keep Israeli forces in Lebanon “as long as the need requires”. “The Iranian regime has been dealt a severe blow, and our policy is clear that Iran will not possess nuclear weapons, whether with or without an agreement. articles.title: Why Fed Cuts Beat Rate Hikes
articles.summary: Markets may be pricing yesterday's economy. iCapital's Dan Suzuki joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why slowing job growth and cooling inflation make Fed rate cuts more likely than hikes, why higher Treasury yields haven't broken the stock rally, and why AI leadership may be shifting away from chipmakers toward hyperscalers that can actually monetize their massive investments. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: No major damage from Iran attacks, US official says
articles.summary: No major damage from Iran attacks, US official says Dozens of missiles and drones fired by Iran caused no significant damage or injuries to American personnel, AFP reported on Thursday, citing a US defence official speaking on condition of anonymity. The official added that Iranian strikes “were intercepted or failed to cause major damage”, adding that there were “no US injuries”.

articles.title: GCC, UAE condemns Iranian attacks in region
articles.summary: GCC, UAE condemns Iranian attacks in region The Gulf of Cooperation Council (GCC) released a statement on Thursday issuing a condemnation of Iranian attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz and holding Iran responsible for threatening global energy security and trade, and breaching the terms of the memorandum of understanding with the US. The GCC also called on the Security Council to take a firm stance to ensure freedom of navigation in the strait and demanded that Iran immediately and unconditionally end all hostilities, without mentioning the US and Israel's role in the conflict. articles.title: Israel ready to attack Iran ‘with even greater force’ if needed, says defence minister
articles.summary: Israel ready to attack Iran ‘with even greater force’ if needed, says defence minister Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz said on Thursday that his country was prepared to resume military operations against Iran for “a third time if necessary”. “The army is ready and on alert for a resumption of fighting, in order to regain air superiority and strike again… in Iran, to eliminate threats, including a third time if necessary,” Katz said at a military ceremony, adding, “If we have to go back, we will go back, with even greater force.”

articles.summary: Iran fired ten ballistic missiles on Jordan's Azraq military base Iran fired 10 ballistic missiles at Jordan's Azraq military base on Thursday, Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said in a statement carried by state media. Jordan said earlier that it had intercepted eight missiles launched from Iran, with no casualties or damage reported, according to the state news agency. The IRGC said American bases in the region would be targeted if US aggression was repeated.
